title: If I Cant Be Yours Lyrics Analysis Thanatos POV
display_title: If I Cant Be Yours Lyrics Analysis Thanatos POV
wikitext:
[]: "If I Can't Be Yours" Lyrics Analysis/Thanatos POV             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Sun Mar 24, 2013 10:53 pm I've been listening to "If I Can't Be Yours" a lot lately, and in particular, the context that songs plays within the movie and timeframe it was made under Gainax. Not the lyrics where it's obvious that it's a women speaking about a love she once had with someone else (like "'Cause every breath that I take/I breath it for you" and "I couldn't face my life without you"/"And I'm so afraid"), but the ones where no lovers or anyone is specifically mentioned. The following are the ones that stick one to me the most: I've been holding it back so long/ But something strange/deep inside of me is happening/ I feel unlike I've ever felt and it's making me scared/ That I may not be what I am think I am/ I don't sleep, don't feel a thing/ And my senses have all but gone/ Can't even cry from the pain/can't shed a tear now/ I've realized we're not the same/ And it's making me sad/ Cause we can't fulfill our dream/ In this life So I must let us break free/ I can never be what you need/ If there was a way through the hurt/ Then I would find it/ I'd take the blows, yes I would fight it/ But this is the one/ Impossible Dream to live Compared to some of the other lyrics, the syntax of each verses all deal with promises/truths the singer kept to itself the whole time, and is only now explaining it to another entity. In the case of placing the song the credits, it's almost like the singer is directly speaking to the audience. Specifically, it's some emotion that just occurred to the singer and is frightened to speak about it. Once it is spoken, the singer is willing to accept that emotion, the consequence it entails, and move on from it all, learning from that same emotion. I want to say it's whoever wrote the lyrics is speaking in some way to Gainax, or really Anno, and the message is clear: they are outright telling anyone watching the movie the mindset of what the movie represents, or for that matter, a reason for why the direction the show took in the second cour. And there's proof that production on EOE was not a happy one (i.e. deadlines not being made, Tsurumaki directing ep 25 of EOE with detachment, ideas being discharged due to technology/time they didn't have). Even Anno wrote once that he was an emotional wreck after EOE was done, and there is a report that he was thinking about ending it all before changing his mind. Such material make it compelling to link the song to any feelings Anno had at the time, but it's probably a little more simpler than that. Thoughts? I'll probably write a less speculative analysis one of these days, but it's one of those fan theories that once it's in your head, you have to ponder.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Mar 24, 2013 11:29 pm Umm... you do realize it's about Rei, right? I thought that was incredibly obvious.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Mar 25, 2013 5:58 pm FWIW, the official Masayuki music video had this song about Misato: http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/2740/Masayukis-Music-Videos/ Old thread where I'm a newbie. I think CAP's alternate interpretation is an interesting one, but I dunno how accurate it is. Loren and Mash performed the song while the composer was Shiro Sagisu of course. I can't find anywhere online if there was some sort of English adaption like the 2 Mike Wyzgowski pieces (Komm, Susser Tod and Everything You've Ever Dreamed) or Sagisu himself wrote the song in English. Don't have my Refrain of Evangelion CD with me, bah.

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Mon Mar 25, 2013 7:00 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: I think CAP's alternate interpretation is an interesting one, but I dunno how accurate it is. Loren and Mash performed the song while the composer was Shiro Sagisu of course. I can't find anywhere online if there was some sort of English adaption like the 2 Mike Wyzgowski pieces (Komm, Susser Tod and Everything You've Ever Dreamed) or Sagisu himself wrote the song in English. Don't have my Refrain of Evangelion CD with me, bah.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Mon Mar 25, 2013 10:27 pm Huh, that's quite observant, i never would have considered viewing some of the lyrics of the song in a more ambiguous light, kudos. I agree with most of the assesment paragraph, only with the exception of: "Once it is spoken, the singer is willing to accept that emotion, the consequence it entails, and move on from it all, learning from that same emotion." Since the song always held a strong tone of resignation and finality within it. That no matter what the singer does (and the singer's willing to do anything), the singer won't be able to surpass this "hurt" or harsh truth. My interpretation differs from the OP in that i don't feel the song's lyrics are from the singer directed to GAINAX or the audience, but that this GAINAX/Anno's feelings directed to no one in particular. I don't know much about the story behind NGE, but as you mention it seems as if production of the original Evangelion series was a rather, eh, intense experience. Somehow, the song's lyrics make me think of some struggling with the realization that they have come too far from the person they used to be to the point that there are irreconciliable differences with this person and their recent way of life. Although i can't say i'm still very sure what is this fatalistic, frightening emotion about ("I've been holding it back so long(...)") Although of course, there's also the matter of translations, figuring out who at GAINAX is responsible for the song, etc. When i get some sleep i should try to see if i can find out something. Also, never realized the original video had Misato. I too always assumed the song was more from Rei's POV than anything else.

[]:             Quote Post by Maru » Tue Mar 26, 2013 1:46 pm Well, I never thought about it quite as deeply as CAP did, but I always thought it was about Asuka, myself. "I fear to tell, I've been holding it back so long" was interpreted by me as Asuka being afraid to admit that she allowed herself to fall in love with Shinji, and that she's been holding it back and lying to herself and the world all this while, about a lot of things but especially about how she feels about him. "But something strange deep inside of me is happening, I feel unlike I ever felt" I read as her reaching a point where she can no longer deny what she's feeling. She can't keep lying to herself anymore that it's just stupid teenage hormones and infatuation. "Are we both from a different world?" I interpreted as Asuka ruminating on the seemingly uncrossable gulf between her and Shinji, how very different they are in so many ways (an echo of Kaji's "woman far away" comments). "There's no one to comfort us" is pretty obvious. Both Asuka and Shinji have lost their mothers. Their father's were abject failures at being parents. They aren't close enough to any peers to share their pain. They can't even comfort each other. "What am I if I can't be yours?" is an echo of Asuka's line "If I can't have you for me alone I don't want you at all." "I realize we're not the same" is again an invocation of how different Asuka and Shinji are despite their similarities. "Cause we can't fulfill our dream in this life" is Asuka lamenting that ultimately she was unable to achieve any of the things she wanted either consciously or unconsciously. She never became an elite ace pilot who fought the enemies of mankind, ripped their skulls from their spines, and tossed them away laughing. She never got Kaji to see her as an adult. She never got Shinji to take the initiative, and never admitted that she was in love with him. In the last stanza, she seems to be saying that the best thing they can do is bid each other farewell, because while she wants him deeply, she feels that she will never be what he wants or needs, and thus that the whole thing is a one sided love affair (based on the seemingly firm evidence of his reactions to her so far -- not breaking the "wall of Jericho," not holding her during the kiss, etc.). She shows a glimmer of her old fire when she says that if she thought there was even the smallest way through, she would try that way, but she simply cannot see any plausible way to break through any more, and so she is letting this go as gracefully as she can, consoling herself by saying that it was an impossible dream. With all that said, I can also see how the lyrics could easily be applied to Rei, especially the first and last stanzas.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Wed Mar 27, 2013 11:56 am I analyzed it once, and came to the conclusion that Rei was in love with Shinji. I'll dig it up out of my mind when i'm not at school.

[]:             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Thu Jan 01, 2015 10:15 pm NemZ wrote: Umm... you do realize it's about Rei, right? I thought that was incredibly obvious.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Jan 02, 2015 1:58 am Tumbling Down wrote: The last two people we saw were Shinji and Asuka.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Jan 02, 2015 5:34 am Its in the end credits of part one, right after GNR shows up and right before 3I starts. The song is about her accepting the role of Lilith and struggling with the implications of this choice.

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Fri Jan 02, 2015 6:35 am Would these be the lyrics by any chance?: I don't sleep, don't feel a thing And my senses have all but gone Can't even cry from the pain, can't shed a tear now I realise We're not the same and it's making me sad Cause we can't fufill our dreams (in the life) So I must, let us break free I can never be what you need If there was a way, through the hurt then I would fint it I'd take the blows. Yes I would fight it but this is the one. Impossible dream to love What am I, if I can't be yours.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Jan 02, 2015 6:38 am ^ Those lyrics are extremely sad when tied to Rei when she goes GNR: she finally found her motivation to finally live, but realize that she still can't escape her duty as Seed of Life and have to separate herself from everyone she has gone to cherish.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Jan 02, 2015 7:01 am Doubly sad when you realize she's only saying it can't happen in THIS life... hence instrumentality. And then Shinji leaves her again. That said, I'd also entertain the notion that this is actually Gendo's thoughts on all his plans comming to nothing... but female vocalist makes that a bit of a hard sell.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Jan 02, 2015 7:21 am NemZ wrote: That said, I'd also entertain the notion that this is actually Gendo's thoughts on all his plans comming to nothing... but female vocalist makes that a bit of a hard sell.

[]:             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Sat Jan 03, 2015 12:24 am NemZ wrote: Umm... you do realize it's about Rei, right? I thought that was incredibly obvious.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Jan 03, 2015 1:12 am I've personally never attached specific characters to these lyrics. The themes dealt with in the lyrics deliver a message that seem to represent a lot of what Evangelion as a whole series has to offer, so I tend to just take it as that. However, if you were to try to apply it to one specific character, Rei is the top contender. The term itself, Thanatos, reflects Rei's attitude towards existence; on a couple occasions she expresses the desire to return to nothingness. When looking at the lyrics with Rei in consideration, it definitely seems to be about her realizing her true nature as Lilith, the implications of her accepting that role, and how much sadness it causes her.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Jan 03, 2015 3:45 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: FWIW, the official Masayuki music video had this song about Misato: http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/2740/Masayukis-Music-Videos/ Old thread where I'm a newbie.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Jan 04, 2015 8:23 am Huh... it's been quite a while since I watched EoE I guess. The connection of the song to pivotal Rei moments throughout the series is still easily strong enough to insist on it being about her though.
